title: PRÉVOST REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Prévost reaction describing anti dihydroxylation of alkenes through iodine and silver carboxylates, giving trans-1,2-diols under anhydrous conditions and cis-1,2-diols under the Woodward-Brutcher modification in the presence of water or acetic acid.
notes: Key concepts: iodoniun-ion formation, silver carboxylate participation, trans-dibenzoate intermediates, and control of trans versus cis vicinal diol stereochemistry through reaction hydration state.

title: PRÉVOST REACTION
summary: Application page for the Prévost reaction featuring trans-7,8-dihydroxy-7,8-dihydrobenzo[a]pyrene phenolic derivatives, (−)-SS20846A, (±)-momilactone A, and (±)-2,3-dihydrofratiglisin C, illustrating both dry Prévost and Woodward-Brutcher conditions for stereocontrolled vicinal diol installation in polycyclic aromatic, piperidine, and terpene syntheses.
notes: Examples emphasize silver benzoate or silver acetate with iodine, dry benzene versus wet acetic acid conditions, β-iodobenzoate intermediates, and late-stage diol installation in dense natural-product frameworks.

title: PRILEZHAEV REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Prilezhaev reaction describing peroxyacid epoxidation of isolated alkenes, a stereospecific syn oxygen transfer that is widely used to form epoxides with predictable retention of alkene geometry and broad functional-group tolerance.
notes: Key concepts: mCPBA-type epoxidation, electron-rich alkenes reacting fastest, selectivity for isolated double bonds, facial bias from substrate control, and preservation of alkene stereochemistry in the epoxide product.

title: PRILEZHAEV REACTION
summary: Application page for the Prilezhaev reaction featuring (−)-21-isopentenylpaxilline, briarielin F, 11-deoxytetrodotoxin, and epothilone B, highlighting highly diastereoselective mCPBA epoxidations of complex terpenoid and polyketide alkenes in the presence of multiple sensitive functional groups.
notes: Examples emphasize buffered mCPBA conditions, chemoselective oxidation of one alkene in multifunctional settings, and substrate-controlled α/β-face discrimination in late-stage natural-product synthesis.

title: PRINS REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Prins reaction describing acid-catalyzed addition of aldehydes to alkenes to generate allylic alcohols, 3-substituted alcohols, 1,3-dioxanes, and related oxocarbenium-derived products, with product distribution governed by nucleophile availability and carbocation fate.
notes: Key concepts: protonated aldehyde addition across alkenes, Markovnikov-type capture, oxocarbenium ions, branching between 1,3-dioxane formation, 1,3-diol formation, and allylic alcohol products, and use of protic or Lewis acids.

title: PRINS REACTION
summary: Application page for the Prins reaction featuring (+)-chatancin, leucascandrolide A, (−)-centrolobine, and (+)-isocycloseychellene, illustrating transannular, tandem Mukaiyama aldol-Prins, segment-coupling Prins cyclization, and catalyst-free oxodiv e-Prins variants for complex ring construction.
notes: Examples emphasize Me2AlCl-promoted cyclization, BF3·OEt2-mediated Mukaiyama aldol-Prins sequences, SnBr4-assisted all-equatorial tetrahydropyran formation, and oxidative/carbony ene-Prins logic in tricyclic ketone synthesis.

title: PRINS-PINACOL REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Prins-pinacol rearrangement describing tandem condensation of allylic 1,2-diols or allylic acetals with aldehydes or ketones to form highly substituted tetrahydrofurans and annulated acyl tetrahydrofurans through sequential Prins cyclization and pinacol rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: oxocarbenium-ion formation, tetrahydrofuran annulation, β-hydroxy carbocation intermediates, ring expansion or cyclopentanone annulation, and Lewis-acid control of stereoselective tandem skeletal editing.

title: PRINS-PINACOL REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for the Prins-pinacol rearrangement featuring briarielin F, (−)-magellaninone, (−)-citreoviral, and (+)-shahamin K, showing Overman-style tandem cyclizations that build fused and spiro tetrahydrofuran motifs with installation of quaternary stereocenters in terpenoid and alkaloid synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize low-temperature acid-promoted acetalization, SnCl4-mediated rearrangement, enantioenriched substrate transfer, and thio-Prins-pinacol variants for hydroazulene core formation.

title: PUMMERER REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Pummerer rearrangement describing activation of sulfoxides with acylating or silylating agents to generate sulfur-stabilized cationic intermediates that are trapped by nucleophiles to give α-substituted sulfides or, after hydrolysis, carbonyl compounds and thiols.
notes: Key concepts: acylsulfonium formation, sulfur-substituted carbocations, α-functionalization of sulfoxides, acid or base co-catalysis, and multiple variants including asymmetric, sila-, additive-, and vinylogous Pummerer rearrangements.

title: PUMMERER REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for the Pummerer rearrangement featuring an enantiopure pyrrolidine, exo-spirotenonate, (±)-deethylibophyllidine, and solanopyrone D, highlighting tandem rearrangement-reduction sequences, acetoxy-aldehyde generation, iminium cyclization, and formyl installation on a pyrone ring.
notes: Examples emphasize TFAA/TFAA activation, Ac2O/NaOAc-promoted rearrangement, Raney-Ni desulfurization, and TMSOTf/TMSNEt2-triggered vinylogous Pummerer chemistry in complex-target synthesis.
